notes: John Kay's own about page: 'establish a consulting company, London Economics' alongside taking the LBS chair in 1986. Co-founder Nick Morris attribution from secondary source.
CH 04083204 incorporated as HAMSARD 2219 LIMITED (shelf company).
Renamed NEW LONDON ECONOMICS LIMITED.
Renamed LONDON ECONOMICS LIMITED — present trading name.
NYC and DC offices, 17 staff. Advised by Alfred E. Kahn.
Marsh & McLennan acquires NERA.
Spin-out from University of Oxford economics department.
Colin Mayer becomes chairman of Oxera (through 2010).
Management buy-out led by Helen Jenkins and Luis Correia da Silva.
Per Frontier's own About: 'Frontier Economics Ltd was formed in June 1999.' Companies House incorporation may predate (some blog text says May); the firm dates itself to June.
Philip Burns joins board.
Sarah Hogg becomes chair.
Michael Webb joins board.
Zoltan Biro joins board.
Founded July 1999 by Roger Bootle, ex-HSBC chief economist.
LDC takes minority stake. First PE investment in a UK economics consultancy.
CRA press release announces 'official opening' of London office: ~15 staff, three VPs, three practices (financial services, telecoms/media, competition).
16-economist walkout from NERA London including Andrea Lofaro.
RBB Economics LLP OC315356 incorporated three-and-a-half years after the firm began trading.
FTI acquires Lexecon for ~$130m cash.
FTI acquires Competition Policy Associates for $72m cash + FTI stock.
FTI merges Lexecon + COMPASS into Compass Lexecon LLC.
